101	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. The one exception is the review agent: if it is **certain** the sync got a marker wrong (a clearly-finished proof left unmarked, or a marker present on something incomplete), it may manually correct it, but must explicitly surface and justify that override in its summary (see `.archon/prompts/review.md`).

The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
110	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
111	
112	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
113	
114	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
115	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true or important at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
116	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
117	
118	## Agent Roles
119	
120	### Plan Agent
121	
122	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
123	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
124	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
125	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
126	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
127	- Curate `ARCHON_MEMORY.md` — the condensed, cross-iteration knowledge file injected (read-only) into every agent's prompt. Keep only durable facts that would surprise an agent reading the code fresh (dead-end tactics, files not to touch, Mathlib gap coordinates, protected invariants). Hard limits: ~10 bullets / ~600 chars; prune before adding. Do NOT duplicate what's obvious from the code or PROGRESS.md.
128	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
129	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
130	
131	### Prover Agent
132	
133	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
134	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
135	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
136	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
137	- A prover lane ends by writing its task result and exiting normally. **Do not kill Lean, Lake, shell, or sibling agent processes from inside the prover lane.** Do not run `pkill`, `killall`, regex-based process cleanup, or background-verification cleanup. If a verification command is still running, wait for its result or report that it is still running; process lifetime is owned by the Archon runner/orchestrator.
138	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
139	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
140	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
141	
142	### Subagents
143	
144	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
145	
146	### Review Agent
147	
148	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
149	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
150	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
151	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
152	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Normally leave `\leanok` to the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase; only override a marker the sync got demonstrably wrong, and justify it in your summary.
153	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
154	
155	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
156	
157	These run automatically between phases:
158	
159	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
160	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
161	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

579	sheaf `F`, the *Čech nerve* is the augmented cosimplicial object of `O_X`-modules whose
580	object in simplicial degree `p` is the product, over the `(p+1)`-tuples `(i₀,…,i_p)` of
581	indices, of the direct images `(j_{i₀…i_p})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})` of the restriction of
582	`F` to the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{i₀…i_p} = U_{i₀} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{i_p}` along the open
583	immersion `j_{i₀…i_p} : U_{i₀…i_p} ↪ X`. Faces are the restriction maps that omit one
584	index, degeneracies repeat one index, and the augmentation in degree `-1` is `F` itself
585	on all of `X`. When `X` is separated each intersection `U_{i₀…i_p}` is affine.
586	
587	This is now *constructed*, not postulated: the geometric backbone `coverCechNerveOverAug`
588	(the augmented Čech nerve of the cover as an augmented simplicial object in `Over X`,
589	unconditional because `Scheme` has all finite limits) is read contravariantly
590	(`SimplicialObject.Augmented.rightOp`) and whiskered by the push–pull functor
591	`pushPullFunctor F = G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`. The whiskering
592	transports both the cosimplicial structure and the augmentation, so the augmentation
593	point is `G` applied to the terminal `Over X`-object `⟨X, 𝟙 X⟩`, namely
594	`(𝟙 X)_* (𝟙 X)^* F ≅ F`. The functor laws of `G` (`pushPullMap_id`, `pushPullMap_comp`)
595	are what make this assembly legitimate.
596	
597	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
598	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
599	noncomputable def CechNerve (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
600	    CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules :=
601	  (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.whiskeringObj (Over X)ᵒᵖ X.Modules (pushPullFunctor F)).obj
602	    (coverCechNerveOverAug 𝒰).rightOp
603	
604	/-- **Relative {\v C}ech complex from a cosimplicial nerve** (coherence-free
605	plumbing). Given `f : X ⟶ S` and an augmented cosimplicial object `N` of
606	`O_X`-modules, produce the relative {\v C}ech cochain complex in `QCoh(S)` by:
607	forgetting the augmentation (`CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop`), pushing the
608	cosimplicial object forward along `f` (`CosimplicialObject.whiskering` applied to
609	`Scheme.Modules.pushforward f`), and taking the alternating coface-map cochain
610	complex (`alternatingCofaceMapComplex`). This is the entire passage `CechNerve ↦
611	CechComplex`, and it uses no `pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` coherence — only
612	the (pre)additivity of `S.Modules`. Project-local. -/
613	noncomputable def relativeCechComplexOfNerve (f : X ⟶ S)
614	    (N : CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules) : CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
615	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex S.Modules).obj
616	    (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering X.Modules S.Modules).obj
617	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f)).obj (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N))
618	
619	/-- **Relative Čech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf** (Stacks, Cohomology of
620	Schemes, `lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`).
621	
622	For `f : X ⟶ S`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X` (with all intersections
623	affine, e.g. `X` separated) and a quasi-coherent sheaf `F`, the *relative Čech
624	complex* `Č•(𝒰, F)` is the cochain complex of `O_S`-modules with degree-`p` term
625	```
626	  Čᵖ(𝒰, F) = ∏_{(i₀,…,i_p)} (f|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}}),
627	```
628	and differential the alternating sum of the restriction maps
629	`(d s)_{i₀…i_{p+1}} = Σⱼ (-1)ʲ s_{i₀…î_j…i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i₀…i_{p+1}}}`. Over an
630	affine `U = Spec A` with `F|_U = M~` and a standard cover by the `D(fᵢ)`, this is
631	the complex of localisations `∏ M_{f_{i₀}} → ∏ M_{f_{i₀}f_{i₁}} → ⋯`. Each term
632	is quasi-coherent because the intersections are affine and the pushforward of a
633	quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated morphism is
634	quasi-coherent.
635	
636	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
637	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
638	noncomputable def CechComplex (f : X ⟶ S) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
639	    CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
640	  -- Construction (Stacks): apply the relative pushforward `f_*` over each finite
641	  -- intersection to the Čech nerve `CechNerve 𝒰 F`, then take the alternating-sum
642	  -- Čech differential. This is exactly the coherence-free plumbing
643	  -- `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`, so `CechComplex` is genuinely *defined* in terms
644	  -- of the nerve: an axiom-clean `CechNerve` immediately yields an axiom-clean
645	  -- `CechComplex`. The only remaining hole is `CechNerve` itself.
646	  relativeCechComplexOfNerve f (CechNerve 𝒰 F)
647	
648	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the augmented Čech complex on `X`
649	
650	The lemma `cechAugmented_exact` (`lem:cech_augmented_resolution`) asserts that the
651	augmented {\v C}ech complex `0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` of a quasi-coherent sheaf `F`
652	over an affine open cover `𝒰` is **exact** in `QCoh(X)` — i.e. the {\v C}ech nerve
653	is a resolution of `F`. This is the *un-pushed* (over `X`, not relative to `f`)
654	counterpart of `CechComplex`/`relativeCechComplexOfNerve`. We record here the
655	structural ingredients that the exactness statement is phrased against: the
656	un-augmented {\v C}ech complex on `X` (`cechComplexOnX`), obtained from the {\v C}ech
657	nerve exactly as `relativeCechComplexOfNerve` does but with the *identity*
658	pushforward — i.e. directly, with no `f_*`. -/
659	
660	/-- **The (un-augmented) {\v C}ech cochain complex of `F` on `X`.** This is the
661	alternating-coface-map complex `C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` of the underlying cosimplicial object
662	of the {\v C}ech nerve `CechNerve 𝒰 F` (its augmentation `F` is dropped). It is the
663	`f = 𝟙`/un-pushed analogue of `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`: the same coherence-free
664	plumbing without the relative pushforward `f_*`, so it is the complex whose
665	exactness (after re-adjoining the augmentation `F → C⁰`) is the content of
666	`cechAugmented_exact`. Project-local. -/
667	noncomputable def cechComplexOnX (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
668	    CochainComplex X.Modules ℕ :=
669	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex X.Modules).obj
670	    (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F))
671	
672	/-- **The augmentation point of the {\v C}ech nerve is `F`.** The augmentation
673	object `(CechNerve 𝒰 F).left` of the {\v C}ech nerve is `(𝟙 X)_* (𝟙 X)^* F` (the
674	push–pull functor applied to the terminal `Over X`-object `⟨X, 𝟙 X⟩`), canonically
675	isomorphic to `F` via the unitors `pushforwardId`/`pullbackId`. Project-local. -/
676	noncomputable def cechNervePointIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
677	    (CechNerve 𝒰 F).left ≅ F :=
678	  (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId X).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 X)).obj F) ≪≫
679	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X).app F
680	
681	/-- **The augmentation map `ε : F → C⁰` of the {\v C}ech nerve.** Built from the
682	augmentation natural transformation of the augmented cosimplicial object
683	`CechNerve 𝒰 F` (the map `(CechNerve 𝒰 F).left ⟶ C⁰`), pre-composed with the unitor
684	`cechNervePointIso` identifying the augmentation point with `F`. This is the map
685	prepended to `cechComplexOnX` to form the augmented {\v C}ech complex. Project-local. -/
686	noncomputable def cechAugmentation (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
687	    F ⟶ (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).X 0 :=
688	  (cechNervePointIso 𝒰 F).inv ≫ (CechNerve 𝒰 F).hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0)
689	
690	/-- **Augmentation kills the first alternating-coface differential** (abstract form).
691	For any augmented cosimplicial object `N` in a preadditive category, the augmentation
692	map `N.hom.app [0] : N.left ⟶ (drop N).obj [0]` followed by the degree-0 alternating
693	coface differential `objD (drop N) 0 = δ⁰ - δ¹` is zero. This is the cosimplicial
694	augmentation identity (`ε ≫ δ⁰ = ε ≫ δ¹`, from naturality of `N.hom` against the two
695	coface maps `[0] ⟶ [1]`); stated abstractly so the proof does not unfold any concrete
696	(heavily whiskered) nerve. Project-local. -/
697	private lemma augmentation_comp_alternatingCofaceMap_objD_zero
698	    {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C] (N : CosimplicialObject.Augmented C) :
699	    N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) ≫
700	      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
701	        (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N) 0 = 0 := by
702	  -- Ascribe the augmentation's codomain to the `𝟭`-free form `N.right.obj ⦋0⦌`; this is
703	  -- the `Comma`'s right functor being `𝟭`, which otherwise pins the composition's middle
704	  -- object to `(𝟭).obj N.right` and blocks every additive distribution lemma's instance.
705	  have hnat : ∀ i : Fin 2,
706	      (N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) : N.left ⟶ N.right.obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) ≫
707	        N.right.δ i = N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 1) := by
708	    intro i
709	    have h := N.hom.naturality (SimplexCategory.δ i)
710	    simpa using h.symm
711	  show (N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) : N.left ⟶ N.right.obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) ≫
712	      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD N.right 0 = 0
713	  simp only [AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD]
714	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_two]
715	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, pow_zero, pow_one, one_zsmul, neg_one_zsmul]
716	  -- `erw` (defeq-matching) is needed: the `Comma`'s right functor `𝟭` pins the
717	  -- composition's middle object to `(𝟭).obj N.right`, so `comp_add`/`comp_neg`'s
718	  -- instances only match up to reducible defeq, not syntactically.
719	  erw [Preadditive.comp_add, Preadditive.comp_neg, hnat 0, hnat 1, add_neg_cancel]
720	
721	/-- **The augmentation composes to zero with `d⁰`.** The {\v C}ech augmentation
722	`ε : F → C⁰` followed by the first {\v C}ech differential `d⁰ : C⁰ → C¹` is zero,
723	the cochain-complex condition needed to prepend `ε` to `cechComplexOnX`. It is the
724	cosimplicial augmentation identity: `ε ≫ δ⁰ = ε ≫ δ¹` (naturality of the
725	augmentation natural transformation against the two coface maps `[0] ⟶ [1]`), so the
726	alternating sum `ε ≫ (δ⁰ - δ¹)` vanishes. Project-local. -/
727	lemma cechAugmentation_comp_d (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
728	    cechAugmentation 𝒰 F ≫ (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).d 0 1 = 0 := by
729	  rw [cechAugmentation, Category.assoc]
730	  have hd : (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).d 0 1 =
731	      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
732	        (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)) 0 := rfl
733	  rw [hd]
734	  erw [augmentation_comp_alternatingCofaceMap_objD_zero (CechNerve 𝒰 F)]
735	  exact Limits.comp_zero
736	
737	/-- **The augmented {\v C}ech complex on `X`** (Stacks
738	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`). The cochain complex
739	`0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` obtained from the un-augmented {\v C}ech complex `cechComplexOnX`
740	by prepending the augmentation `ε : F → C⁰` (`cechAugmentation`) in degree `0` — so
741	`(cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).X 0 = F` and `(cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).X (p+1) = Cᵖ`. The
742	augmentation condition `ε ≫ d⁰ = 0` is `cechAugmentation_comp_d`. The lemma
743	`cech_augmented_resolution` (`cechAugmented_exact`) asserts this complex is exact —
744	i.e. the {\v C}ech nerve is a resolution of `F` in `QCoh(X)`. Project-local. -/
745	noncomputable def cechAugmentedComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
746	    CochainComplex X.Modules ℕ :=
747	  (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).augment (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F) (cechAugmentation_comp_d 𝒰 F)
748	
749	/-- **The Čech complex computes the higher direct images** (Stacks 02KE;
750	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent` and
751	`lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application`).
752	
753	Let `f : X ⟶ S` be separated and quasi-compact, `F` a quasi-coherent
754	`O_X`-module, and `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X` (so, by separatedness,
755	every intersection is affine). Then the cohomology sheaves of the relative Čech
756	complex compute the higher direct images: for every `i ≥ 0` there is a canonical
757	isomorphism of `O_S`-modules
758	```
759	  Hⁱ(Č•(𝒰, F)) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F.
760	```
761	In particular, over an affine base `S = Spec A`, taking global sections gives
762	`Hⁱ(X, F) = Čⁱ(𝒰, F) = H⁰(S, Rⁱ f_* F)` as `A`-modules.
763	
764	We state the isomorphism as `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` and compare against the
765	derived-functor higher direct image `higherDirectImage` wherever the latter is
766	defined (`HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules`).
767	
768	Route A: reduce to `S` affine; the augmented Čech complex is a termwise
769	`f_*`-acyclic resolution of `F` (each term is a product of pushforwards over
770	affine intersections, acyclic by `CechAcyclic.affine` via the P3b bridge), so
771	the P4 acyclic-resolution lemma (`rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`) gives
772	`Hⁱ(f_* C•) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F`. -/
773	theorem cech_computes_higherDirectImage [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]
774	    (f : X ⟶ S) [QuasiCompact f] [IsSeparated f] (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
775	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) (i : ℕ) :
776	    Nonempty ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F) := by
777	  -- Route A: reduce to `S` affine; the Čech complex is a termwise acyclic
778	  -- resolution of `F` (by `CechAcyclic.affine`), so `rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`
779	  -- gives `Hⁱ(f_* C•) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F`.
780	  sorry
781	
782	end AlgebraicGeometry

784	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sheafification annihilates a locally-zero presheaf
785	
786	These three site-theoretic lemmas are the reusable **Step 2** of the
787	sections/sheafification route to {\v C}ech-resolution exactness
788	(`lem:cech_augmented_resolution`): the homology sheaf of a complex of sheaves of
789	modules is the sheafification of the *presheaf* homology
790	(`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`), and that sheafification is the zero
791	object whenever the presheaf homology is *locally zero* — i.e. it receives a local
792	isomorphism (`J.W`-equivalence) from, or is locally bijective onto, a presheaf that
793	is objectwise zero.
794	
795	They are stated for a general Grothendieck topology and an arbitrary abelian
796	(concrete) target, depend only on Mathlib, and live in this — the most upstream
797	`Cohomology` file — so that the downstream exactness argument (whose section-level
798	vanishing input `sectionCech_affine_vanishing` and the bridge
799	`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify` both reside in files that *import* this one)
800	can consume them without an import cycle. -/
801	
802	namespace CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology
803	
804	variable {C : Type*} [Category C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
805	
806	/-- **Sheafification transports the zero-object property across a `J.W`-equivalence.**
807	If `f : P ⟶ Q` is a local isomorphism (`J.W f`) and the sheafification of `Q` is a
808	zero object, then so is the sheafification of `P`. The local isomorphism becomes an
809	isomorphism after sheafification (`GrothendieckTopology.W_iff`), and zero objects are
810	stable under isomorphism. Project-local Mathlib supplement (site theory). -/
811	lemma isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W {A : Type*} [Category A] [HasWeakSheafify J A]
812	    {P Q : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : P ⟶ Q) (hf : J.W f)
813	    (hQ : Limits.IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J A).obj Q)) :
814	    Limits.IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J A).obj P) := by
815	  have : IsIso ((presheafToSheaf J A).map f) := (J.W_iff f).1 hf
816	  exact hQ.of_iso (asIso ((presheafToSheaf J A).map f))
817	
818	/-- **Sheafification of a presheaf locally isomorphic to a zero presheaf is zero.**
819	If `f : P ⟶ Q` is a `J.W`-equivalence and `Q` is *objectwise* a zero object, then the
820	sheafification of `P` is the zero object. (Sheafification is additive over an abelian
821	target — `presheafToSheaf_additive` — so it carries the zero presheaf `Q` to a zero
822	sheaf; combine with `isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W`.) This is the form the
823	{\v C}ech-resolution argument applies to the presheaf homology `V ↦ Ȟᵖ(V, F)`, which is
824	locally zero on the affine basis. Project-local Mathlib supplement (site theory). -/
825	lemma isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W_isZero {A : Type*} [Category A] [Abelian A]
826	    [HasSheafify J A] {P Q : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : P ⟶ Q) (hf : J.W f) (hQ : Limits.IsZero Q) :
827	    Limits.IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J A).obj P) := by
828	  haveI : (presheafToSheaf J A).Additive := presheafToSheaf_additive
829	  exact isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W J f hf ((presheafToSheaf J A).map_isZero hQ)
830	
831	/-- **Sheafification of a presheaf locally bijective onto a zero presheaf is zero.**
832	Concrete-target form of `isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W_isZero`: when the topology
833	satisfies `WEqualsLocallyBijective` (e.g. `AddCommGrpCat`), a map `f : P ⟶ Q` that is
834	locally injective and locally surjective is a `J.W`-equivalence
835	(`GrothendieckTopology.W_of_isLocallyBijective`); if moreover `Q` is objectwise zero,
836	the sheafification of `P` vanishes. The downstream consumer obtains the local
837	injectivity from the basis-vanishing of the section {\v C}ech homology and the local
838	surjectivity for free (the target is a zero presheaf). Project-local Mathlib
839	supplement (site theory). -/
840	lemma isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective {A : Type*} [Category A]
841	    {FA : A → A → Type*} {CA : A → Type*} [(X Y : A) → FunLike (FA X Y) (CA X) (CA Y)]
842	    [ConcreteCategory A FA] [Abelian A] [HasSheafify J A] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective A]
843	    {P Q : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : P ⟶ Q) [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J f]
844	    [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J f] (hQ : Limits.IsZero Q) :
845	    Limits.IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J A).obj P) :=
846	  isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W_isZero J f (J.W_of_isLocallyBijective f) hQ
847	
848	end CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology

61	    (G : C ⥤ D) [G.Additive] (Y : CosimplicialObject C) :
62	    (G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
63	        ((AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex C).obj Y) ≅
64	      (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex D).obj
65	        (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering C D).obj G).obj Y) :=
66	  HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents (fun _ => Iso.refl _) (by
67	    rintro i j (rfl : i + 1 = j)
68	    simp only [Iso.refl_hom, Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id,
69	      Functor.mapHomologicalComplex_obj_d, AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex_obj]
70	    dsimp only [AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.obj]
71	    rw [CochainComplex.of_d, CochainComplex.of_d]
72	    simp only [AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD, Functor.map_sum,
73	      Functor.map_zsmul]
74	    rfl)

76	/-- **The `f_*`-image of the un-augmented Čech complex on `X` is isomorphic to the relative Čech
77	complex** (blueprint `lem:pushforward_mapHC_cechComplexOnX`). -/
78	noncomputable def pushforward_mapHomologicalComplex_cechComplexOnX
79	    (f : X ⟶ S) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
80	    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
81	        (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F) ≅ CechComplex f 𝒰 F :=
82	  -- `cechComplexOnX` and `CechComplex` are *definitionally* the alternating coface complexes of
83	  -- the (un-whiskered, resp. `f_*`-whiskered) underlying cosimplicial object of the Čech nerve,
84	  -- so the general helper applies on the nose.
85	  mapAlternatingCofaceMapComplexIso (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f)
86	    (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F))

88	/-! ## From augmented exactness to the acyclic-resolution input data -/
89	
90	/- Planner strategy: lem:cechAugmented_to_acyclicResolutionInput ·
91	From `cechAugmented_exact` (CechAugmentedResolution.lean) we have:
92	  `∀ p, IsZero ((cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).homology p)`.
93	The augmented complex has `X 0 = F` and `X (n+1) = (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).X n`; its differential at
94	degree 0 is the augmentation `ε : F → C⁰`.
95	
96	(1) Exactness of `cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F` at degree `n+1`:  the augmented complex at degree `n+2`
97	    coincides with the un-augmented complex at degree `n+1`.  Use
98	    `HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology` plus the vanishing from `cechAugmented_exact`.
99	
100	(2) Iso `e : F ≅ (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).cycles 0`:  vanishing of homology at degree 0 gives that
101	    ε is a monomorphism; vanishing at degree 1 gives that the image of ε equals `ker d⁰ = cycles 0`.
102	    Hence ε is an iso onto `cycles 0`.  The iso is assembled from the augmentation `cechAugmentation`
103	    and the exactness data; use `ShortComplex.Exact.isoOfEpiMonoIsZero` or similar.
104	
105	Both outputs are assembled into a `PProd` (anonymous constructor `⟨e, hexact⟩`; `PProd` rather
106	than `Prod` because the second component is a `Prop` while the first is an `Iso` in `Type`). -/
107	/-- **From augmented exactness to the P4 input data**
108	(blueprint `lem:cechAugmented_to_acyclicResolutionInput`).
109	
110	Given the hypotheses of `cechAugmented_exact`, this declaration packages the two pieces of data
111	that `rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` (the abstract acyclic-resolution lemma) requires:
112	an isomorphism `e : F ≅ (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).cycles 0` identifying `F` with the 0-cocycles,
113	and exactness `(cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).ExactAt (n+1)` in every positive degree. -/
114	noncomputable def cechAugmented_to_acyclicResolutionInput
115	    (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (h𝒰 : ∀ i, IsAffine (𝒰.X i)) [X.IsSeparated]
116	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) :
117	    (F ≅ (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).cycles 0) ×' (∀ n, (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).ExactAt (n + 1)) := by
118	  have hKex : ∀ p, (cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).ExactAt p := fun p =>
119	    (HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology _ p).2 (cechAugmented_exact 𝒰 h𝒰 F hF p)
120	  -- (2) positive-degree exactness: the short complex of the augmented complex around `n+2`
121	  -- is definitionally the short complex of the un-augmented complex around `n+1`.
122	  have hexact : ∀ n, (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).ExactAt (n + 1) := by
123	    intro n
124	    have h := hKex (n + 2)
125	    rw [(cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).exactAt_iff' (n + 1) (n + 2) (n + 3)
126	      ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev_eq' rfl) ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).next_eq' rfl)] at h
127	    rw [(cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).exactAt_iff' n (n + 1) (n + 2)
128	      ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev_eq' rfl) ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).next_eq' rfl)]
129	    exact h
130	  -- ε is a monomorphism: the augmented complex is exact at 0 and its incoming map vanishes.
131	  have h0 := hKex 0
132	  rw [(cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).exactAt_iff' 0 0 1 CochainComplex.prev_nat_zero
133	    ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).next_eq' rfl)] at h0
134	  haveI hmono : Mono (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F) :=
135	    h0.mono_g ((cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).shape 0 0 (by simp))
136	  -- exactness of the augmented complex at 1: the short complex `F –ε→ C⁰ –d⁰→ C¹`.
137	  have h1 := hKex 1
138	  rw [(cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).exactAt_iff' 0 1 2
139	    ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev_eq' rfl) ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).next_eq' rfl)] at h1
140	  haveI : Mono ((cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).sc' 0 1 2).f := hmono
141	  -- `ι' ≫ ε = iCycles`: the exactness-lift of the cycle inclusion through ε.
142	  have hl : h1.lift ((cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).iCycles 0) ((cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).iCycles_d 0 1) ≫
143	      cechAugmentation 𝒰 F = (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).iCycles 0 := h1.lift_f _ _
144	  refine ⟨⟨(cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).liftCycles (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F) 1
145	      ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).next_eq' rfl) (cechAugmentation_comp_d 𝒰 F),
146	    h1.lift ((cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).iCycles 0) ((cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).iCycles_d 0 1),
147	    ?_, ?_⟩, hexact⟩
148	  · -- hom_inv_id: cancel the monomorphism ε on the right.
149	    rw [← cancel_mono (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F), Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, hl,
150	      HomologicalComplex.liftCycles_i]
151	  · -- inv_hom_id: cancel the monomorphism `iCycles` on the right.
152	    rw [← cancel_mono ((cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).iCycles 0), Category.assoc, Category.id_comp,
153	      HomologicalComplex.liftCycles_i, hl]
154	
155	/-! ## Capstone: Čech computes higher direct images (affine-cover form) -/
156	
157	/- Planner strategy: lem:cech_computes_cohomology_affineCover ·
158	Assembly of the four Route-A ingredients:
159	
160	(a) `cechAugmented_to_acyclicResolutionInput` yields:
161	    · `e : F ≅ (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).cycles 0`
162	    · `hexact : ∀ n, (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).ExactAt (n+1)`
163	
164	(b) `cechTerm_pushforward_acyclic` provides the typeclass instance:
165	    `[∀ p, (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).IsRightAcyclic ((cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).X p)]`
166	    (introduce with `haveI` for each `p`; or use `inferInstance` if the `∀ p` form is
167	    synthesisable from a blanket instance).
168	
169	(c) `Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` (AcyclicResolution.lean, fully proved) with
170	    G = `Scheme.Modules.pushforward f`, K = `cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F`, A = F, gives:
171	    `((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).rightDerived i).obj F
172	      ≅ ((G.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F)).homology i`
173	
174	(d) `pushforward_mapHomologicalComplex_cechComplexOnX` rewrites the right-hand side to
175	    `(CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i`.
176	
177	The final iso `(CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F` is the composite of
178	(d).symm, (c).symm, noting `higherDirectImage f i F = ((pushforward f).rightDerived i).obj F`.
179	Wrap in `Nonempty` via `⟨iso⟩`.
180	
181	Additive / PreservesFiniteLimits hypotheses on `pushforward f`: `Additive` is an instance;
182	`PreservesFiniteLimits` is needed for `rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` (via
183	`PreservesFiniteLimits (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f)` — left-exact since it is a right adjoint
184	via the global sections adjunction). -/
185	/-- **Čech computes higher direct images — affine-cover form**
186	(blueprint `lem:cech_computes_cohomology_affineCover`; Stacks Tag 02KE).
187	
188	Let `f : X ⟶ S` be a separated quasi-compact morphism with `X` and `S` both separated, `F` a
189	quasi-coherent `O_X`-module, `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X`, and `hres` threading
190	`HasInjectiveResolutions` on each intersection subscheme.  Then for every `i ≥ 0` there is an
191	isomorphism between the `i`-th cohomology of the relative Čech complex and the `i`-th higher
192	direct image:
193	```
194	  (CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ R^i f_* F  =  higherDirectImage f i F.
195	```
196	This is the correctly-stated capstone; the frozen `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` in
197	`CechHigherDirectImage.lean` omits `[X.IsSeparated]` and `h𝒰` and is escalated. -/
198	theorem cech_computes_higherDirectImage_of_affineCover [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]
199	    (f : X ⟶ S) [QuasiCompact f] [IsSeparated f] [X.IsSeparated] [S.IsSeparated]
200	    (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (h𝒰 : ∀ i, IsAffine (𝒰.X i))
201	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) (i : ℕ)
202	    (hres : ∀ (n : ℕ) (σ : Fin (n + 1) → 𝒰.I₀),
203	      HasInjectiveResolutions (Scheme.Opens.toScheme (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)).Modules) :
204	    Nonempty ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F) := by
205	  -- (a) the resolution data extracted from the exactness of the augmented Čech complex
206	  obtain ⟨e, hexact⟩ := cechAugmented_to_acyclicResolutionInput 𝒰 h𝒰 F hF
207	  -- (b) termwise right-`f_*`-acyclicity of the Čech complex on `X` (black box from
208	  -- `CechTermAcyclic.lean`)
209	  haveI : ∀ n, (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).IsRightAcyclic ((cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).X n) :=
210	    fun n => cechTerm_pushforward_acyclic f 𝒰 h𝒰 F hF n (hres n)
211	  -- (c) `f_*` is left exact: it is a right adjoint (of the module pullback)
212	  haveI : PreservesLimits (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f) :=
213	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).rightAdjoint_preservesLimits
214	  -- (d) assemble: P4 acyclic-resolution comparison, then rewrite `f_* C•` to the relative
215	  -- Čech complex; `higherDirectImage f i F` is definitionally `((f_*).rightDerived i).obj F`.
216	  exact ⟨(HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor S.Modules (ComplexShape.up ℕ) i).mapIso
217	      (pushforward_mapHomologicalComplex_cechComplexOnX f 𝒰 F).symm ≪≫
218	    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution
219	      (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F) F e hexact i).symm⟩
220	
221	end AlgebraicGeometry

12	/-! # Čech term acyclicity for the pushforward
13	
14	## IMPORTANT signature correction (iter-077 prover)
15	
16	The originally-planned signature of `cechTerm_pushforward_acyclic` (no hypothesis on `S`) is
17	**mathematically false**.  Counterexample: let `S` be the affine plane with a doubled origin,
18	`X = 𝔸²` and `f : X ⟶ S` the open immersion onto the first copy (an open immersion is separated,
19	and `f` is quasi-compact since everything is noetherian); `X` is affine and separated, so the
20	one-element cover `𝒰 = {𝟙 X}` is a finite affine cover.  Every Čech term is then isomorphic to
21	`F`, so the claimed conclusion specializes to `R^k f_* F = 0` for `k ≥ 1` — but for `F = O_X`
22	the stalk of `R^1 f_* O_X` at the doubled origin `o₂` is
23	`colim_{W ∋ 0} H^1(W \ {0}, O) ≅ H²_𝔪(A) ≠ 0` (`A` the local ring of the plane at the origin).
24	The underlying error in the informal proof: for affine `U ⊆ X` and affine `V ⊆ S`,
25	`U ∩ f⁻¹(V) ≅ U ×_S V` is affine only when the *diagonal of `S`* is affine (e.g. `S`
26	separated); `f` separated does not suffice.  Accordingly the lemma below carries the extra
27	hypothesis `[S.IsSeparated]` (what is really used is that `S` has affine diagonal, so any
28	morphism from an affine scheme to `S` is affine).  The same hypothesis is consequently REQUIRED
29	by the capstone `cech_computes_higherDirectImage_of_affineCover` (same counterexample: the Čech
30	complex of the trivial cover is `f_* F` in degree 0, with vanishing `H^1`, while
31	`R^1 f_* F ≠ 0`).
32	
33	In addition, the proof must pass through the category of modules on the intersection schemes
34	`U_σ = (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).toScheme`, whose `HasInjectiveResolutions` instance is NOT available
35	in Mathlib (`IsGrothendieckAbelian (SheafOfModules R)` is absent — see CechToCohomology.lean);
36	exactly as the frozen target carries `[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]` as a hypothesis, this
37	lemma carries the corresponding hypothesis `hres` for the (finitely many) intersection opens. -/
38	
39	universe u
40	
41	open CategoryTheory Limits
42	
43	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
44	
45	open Scheme.Modules
46	
47	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
48	
49	/-! ## Auxiliary: additivity of the right-derived functor
50	
51	Mathlib defines `Functor.rightDerived` but registers no `Additive` instance for it (nor for
52	`injectiveResolutions`).  We supply both here: two descents of `f + g` between chosen injective
53	resolutions are homotopic, so in the homotopy category the descent of a sum is the sum of the
54	descents; additivity of the right-derived functor follows by composition. -/
55	
56	section RightDerivedAdditive
57	
58	variable {𝒜 ℬ : Type*} [Category 𝒜] [Abelian 𝒜] [HasInjectiveResolutions 𝒜]
59	  [Category ℬ] [Abelian ℬ]
60	
61	/-- The chosen-injective-resolutions functor `𝒜 ⥤ HomotopyCategory 𝒜` is additive: `desc f +
62	desc g` is also a descent of `f + g`, and any two descents are homotopic
63	(`InjectiveResolution.descHomotopy`). Project-local Mathlib supplement. -/
64	instance injectiveResolutions_additive : (injectiveResolutions 𝒜).Additive where
65	  map_add {Z W f g} := by
66	    dsimp only [injectiveResolutions]
67	    refine Eq.trans ?_ ((HomotopyCategory.quotient 𝒜 (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).map_add
68	      (f := InjectiveResolution.desc f (injectiveResolution W) (injectiveResolution Z))
69	      (g := InjectiveResolution.desc g (injectiveResolution W) (injectiveResolution Z)))
70	    apply HomotopyCategory.eq_of_homotopy
71	    apply InjectiveResolution.descHomotopy (f + g)
72	    · exact InjectiveResolution.desc_commutes _ _ _
73	    · rw [Preadditive.comp_add, InjectiveResolution.desc_commutes,
74	        InjectiveResolution.desc_commutes, ← Preadditive.add_comp, ← Functor.map_add]
75	
76	/-- The right-derived functor of an additive functor is additive (composite of the additive
77	`injectiveResolutions`, `mapHomotopyCategory` and homotopy-category homology functors).
78	Project-local Mathlib supplement. -/
79	lemma rightDerived_additive (G : 𝒜 ⥤ ℬ) [G.Additive] (n : ℕ) : (G.rightDerived n).Additive :=
80	  letI : G.rightDerivedToHomotopyCategory.Additive :=
81	    inferInstanceAs (injectiveResolutions 𝒜 ⋙
82	      G.mapHomotopyCategory (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).Additive
83	  inferInstanceAs (G.rightDerivedToHomotopyCategory ⋙
84	    HomotopyCategory.homologyFunctor ℬ (ComplexShape.up ℕ) n).Additive
85	
86	/-- A biproduct of zero objects is zero: the identity equals zero because every component map
87	into a zero object is unique. Project-local helper. -/
88	lemma isZero_biproduct {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasZeroMorphisms C] {J : Type*}
89	    (Z : J → C) [HasBiproduct Z] (h : ∀ j, IsZero (Z j)) : IsZero (⨁ Z) := by
90	  rw [IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero]
91	  apply biproduct.hom_ext
92	  intro j
93	  exact (h j).eq_of_tgt _ _
94	
95	/-- Right-`G`-acyclicity transports along an isomorphism. Project-local helper. -/
96	lemma isRightAcyclic_of_iso (G : 𝒜 ⥤ ℬ) [G.Additive] {A B : 𝒜} (e : A ≅ B)
97	    [G.IsRightAcyclic A] : G.IsRightAcyclic B where
98	  vanish k := (Functor.IsRightAcyclic.vanish (G := G) (J := A) k).of_iso
99	    ((G.rightDerived (k + 1)).mapIso e).symm
100	
101	end RightDerivedAdditive
102	
103	/-! ## Auxiliary: finite products of acyclic objects -/
104	
105	/- Planner strategy: lem:rightAcyclic_finite_prod ·
106	A finite categorical product (= finite biproduct in an abelian category) of right-G-acyclic
107	objects is right-G-acyclic.  Since `G.rightDerived (k+1)` is additive it preserves finite
108	biproducts; each factor `(G.rightDerived (k+1)).obj (X i)` is zero by the per-factor acyclicity
109	instance, so their finite product/biproduct is zero.
110	Proof route: `Functor.IsRightAcyclic.vanish` on the product reduces to
111	`IsZero (∏ᶜ i, (G.rightDerived (k+1)).obj (X i))` (additive right-derived preserves ∏ᶜ), then
112	each factor is zero by `Functor.IsRightAcyclic.vanish i`, and a finite product of zero objects is
113	zero (`IsZero.prod` / `Limits.IsZero.pi`). -/
114	/-- **A finite product of right-`G`-acyclic objects is right-`G`-acyclic**
115	(blueprint `lem:rightAcyclic_finite_prod`).
116	
117	Let `G : 𝒜 ⥤ ℬ` be an additive functor between abelian categories with injective resolutions,
118	and let `(X i)_{i : ι}` be a finite family of objects each right `G`-acyclic.  Then the
119	categorical product `∏ᶜ i, X i` is also right `G`-acyclic. -/
120	lemma rightAcyclic_finite_prod
121	    {𝒜 ℬ : Type*} [Category 𝒜] [Abelian 𝒜] [HasInjectiveResolutions 𝒜]
122	    [HasFiniteProducts 𝒜] [Category ℬ] [Abelian ℬ]
123	    (G : 𝒜 ⥤ ℬ) [G.Additive] {ι : Type*} [Finite ι]
124	    (Xf : ι → 𝒜) [∀ i, G.IsRightAcyclic (Xf i)] :
125	    G.IsRightAcyclic (∏ᶜ fun i => Xf i) := by
126	  constructor
127	  intro k
128	  haveI : (G.rightDerived (k + 1)).Additive := rightDerived_additive G (k + 1)
129	  -- Finite biproducts in abelian categories (a `local instance` in Mathlib, not global).
130	  haveI : HasFiniteBiproducts 𝒜 := Abelian.hasFiniteBiproducts
131	  haveI : HasFiniteBiproducts ℬ := Abelian.hasFiniteBiproducts
132	  -- Reindex along an equivalence `ι ≃ Fin n` so the `Type 0` finite-biproduct instances apply.
133	  obtain ⟨n, ⟨e⟩⟩ := Finite.exists_equiv_fin ι
134	  haveI : ∀ j : Fin n, G.IsRightAcyclic ((Xf ∘ e.symm) j) :=
135	    fun j => inferInstanceAs (G.IsRightAcyclic (Xf (e.symm j)))
136	  -- `∏ᶜ Xf ≅ ∏ᶜ (Xf ∘ e.symm) ≅ ⨁ (Xf ∘ e.symm)`, and the additive right-derived functor
137	  -- takes the biproduct to the biproduct of the (vanishing) per-factor values.
138	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_
139	    ((G.rightDerived (k + 1)).mapIso
140	      (Pi.whiskerEquiv (f := fun i => Xf i) (g := Xf ∘ e.symm) e
141	          (fun i => eqToIso (congrArg Xf (e.symm_apply_apply i))) ≪≫
142	        (biproduct.isoProduct (Xf ∘ e.symm)).symm) ≪≫
143	      (G.rightDerived (k + 1)).mapBiproduct (Xf ∘ e.symm))
144	  exact isZero_biproduct _ fun j =>
145	    Functor.IsRightAcyclic.vanish (G := G) (J := (Xf ∘ e.symm) j) k
146	
147	/-! ## Auxiliary: a morphism from an affine scheme to a separated scheme is affine -/
148	
149	/-- **A morphism from an affine scheme to a separated scheme is affine** (Stacks 01S7).
150	`g ≫ terminal.from S = terminal.from U` is affine because `U` is affine, and `terminal.from S`
151	is separated, so the cancellation property of affine morphisms applies. Project-local (the
152	identical statement exists as a `private` lemma in `OpenImmersionPushforward.lean`). -/
153	lemma isAffineHom_of_isAffine_of_isSeparated {U Z : Scheme.{u}} (g : U ⟶ Z)
154	    [IsAffine U] [Z.IsSeparated] : IsAffineHom g := by
155	  haveI hcomp : IsAffineHom (g ≫ terminal.from Z) := by
156	    have he : g ≫ terminal.from Z = terminal.from U := terminal.hom_ext _ _
157	    rw [he]; infer_instance
158	  exact IsAffineHom.of_comp g (terminal.from Z)
159	
160	/-! ## Relative Serre vanishing for an affine morphism from an affine scheme
161	
162	This is the generalization of `higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic` from affine open
163	immersions into a separated scheme to arbitrary affine morphisms from an affine scheme: the
164	open-immersion hypothesis was only used there to derive `IsAffineHom j`, so we take the latter
165	as the hypothesis.  The proof is the same Stacks 01XJ + Serre-vanishing argument: `R^q j_* H`
166	is the sheafification of `V ↦ H^q(j⁻¹V, H)`, which dies sectionwise on the affine basis since
167	`j⁻¹V` is affine for affine `V`. -/
168	
169	-- Re-activate the (file-local) `HasExt` instance from `AbsoluteCohomology.lean` (same pattern
170	-- as `OpenImmersionPushforward.lean:111`) so the `Ext`-based transport below resolves it without
171	-- the slow `HasSmallLocalizedHom` search (which exceeds even a 1000000-heartbeat budget).
172	attribute [local instance] hasExtModules
173	
174	section AffineHomAcyclic
175	
176	variable {U Z : Scheme.{u}}
177	
178	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 1000000 in

180	/-- **Higher direct images along an affine morphism from an affine scheme vanish**
181	(Stacks `lemma-relative-affine-vanishing`, project-local generalization of
182	`higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic` from open immersions to affine morphisms). -/
183	theorem higherDirectImage_affineHom_acyclic [HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules]
184	    (j : U ⟶ Z) [IsAffineHom j] [IsAffine U]
185	    (H : U.Modules) (hH : H.IsQuasicoherent) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) :
186	    IsZero (higherDirectImage j q H) := by
187	  -- Presheaf description (Stacks 01XJ): `Rᵠ j_* H ≅ sheafify(V ↦ Hᵠ((j_* I•)(V)))`
188	  -- for any injective resolution `I` of `H`. Reduce to the vanishing of that sheafification.
189	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_
190	    (higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology j q (injectiveResolution H))
191	  set α : Z.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ Z.ringCatSheaf.obj := 𝟙 Z.ringCatSheaf.obj with hα
192	  set P := (pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex j (injectiveResolution H)).homology q with hP
193	  -- Reflect `IsZero` through the faithful, zero-preserving forgetful functor `toSheaf`.
194	  apply Functor.isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms (SheafOfModules.toSheaf Z.ringCatSheaf)
195	  -- Transport across the sheafification square
196	  -- `toSheaf ∘ sheafification ≅ presheafToSheaf ∘ toPresheaf`.
197	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf α).app P)
198	  set Q := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf Z.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj P with hQ
199	  -- KEY VANISHING: the presheaf homology `Q` is a *zero object* over every affine open
200	  -- `W ⊆ Z`, because `j⁻¹W` is affine (`j` is an affine morphism) and Serre vanishing
201	  -- (via the `Ext`-transport to the spectrum) kills `Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)` for `q ≥ 1`.
202	  have hSec : ∀ (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens Z), IsAffineOpen W →
203	      IsZero (Q.obj (Opposite.op W)) := by
204	    intro W hW
205	    -- Evaluate-at-`W` the presheaf complex; this functor preserves homology, so
206	    -- `Q.obj (op W) ≅ Hᵠ(Γ(W, j_* I•)) = Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)`, which vanishes for affine `j⁻¹W`.
207	    set GW := PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf Z.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
208	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens Z)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W) with hGW
209	    set Kp := pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex j (injectiveResolution H) with hKp
210	    refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ (GW.mapHomologyIso' (ComplexShape.up ℕ) Kp q).symm
211	    -- The section complex `n ↦ Γ(W, j_* I^n) = Γ(j⁻¹W, I^n)` is the image of the chosen
212	    -- injective resolution of `H` under the additive sections-over-`j⁻¹W` functor.
213	    have hcomplex : (GW.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj Kp
214	        = ((pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
215	            (injectiveResolution H).cocomplex :=
216	      rfl
217	    rw [hcomplex]
218	    -- `isoRightDerivedObj` recognises this homology as the `q`-th right derived sections
219	    -- functor `Rᵠ Γ(j⁻¹W, -)` applied to `H`, i.e. the absolute cohomology `Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)`.
220	    refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ ((injectiveResolution H).isoRightDerivedObj
221	      (pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W) q).symm
222	    -- The sections functor `Γ(j⁻¹W, -)` is corepresented by `jShriekOU (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)`, so its
223	    -- `q`-th right derived functor agrees with that of `Hom(jShriekOU (j ⁻¹ᵁ W), -)`.
224	    refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ ((rightDerivedNatIso (sectionsFunctorCorepIso (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)) q).app H)
225	    -- `isZero_coyoneda_rightDerived_of_forall_ext_eq_zero` reduces the right-derived vanishing
226	    -- of `Hom(jShriekOU (j⁻¹W), -)` to the `Ext`-vanishing.
227	    refine isZero_coyoneda_rightDerived_of_forall_ext_eq_zero (jShriekOU (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)) H q hq ?_
228	    intro e
229	    -- Discharged by `ext_jShriekOU_eq_zero_of_specIso` (the spectrum `Ext`-transport).
230	    refine ext_jShriekOU_eq_zero_of_specIso U U.isoSpec (j ⁻¹ᵁ W) H q hq
231	      (U.isoSpec.inv ⁻¹ᵁ (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)) ?hV' ?hjt ?hqc e
232	    -- `j⁻¹W` is affine (`j` is an affine morphism, `W` affine), and its preimage along the
233	    -- iso `U.isoSpec.inv` is affine.
234	    case hV' => exact (hW.preimage j).preimage_of_isIso U.isoSpec.inv
235	    case hjt => exact jShriekOU_transport_along_iso U.isoSpec (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)
236	    case hqc => exact pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh U.isoSpec H hH
237	  -- Since affine opens form a basis of `Z`, every section of `Q` restricts to `0` on the
238	  -- affine opens contained in its domain, so `Q` is sectionwise locally zero and its
239	  -- sheafification vanishes.
240	  apply CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_sections_locally_zero
241	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) (Q := Q)
242	  intro V s
243	  refine ⟨{ arrows := fun {W'} _ => ∃ W : TopologicalSpace.Opens Z,
244	              IsAffineOpen W ∧ W' ≤ W ∧ W ≤ V,
245	            downward_closed := ?_ }, ?_, ?_⟩
246	  · rintro W' W'' g ⟨A, hA, hWA, hAV⟩ h
247	    exact ⟨A, hA, le_trans (leOfHom h) hWA, hAV⟩
248	  · -- membership in the opens topology: every point of `V` has an affine basis neighbourhood
249	    intro x hx
250	    obtain ⟨W, hWmem, hxW, hWV⟩ :=
251	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.isBasis_iff_nbhd.mp (Scheme.isBasis_affineOpens Z)) hx
252	    exact ⟨W, homOfLE hWV, ⟨W, hWmem, le_refl W, hWV⟩, hxW⟩
253	  · -- the restriction of `s` to a sieve member `W' ≤ A ≤ V` (with `A` affine) factors through
254	    -- `Q.obj (op A) = 0`, hence vanishes.
255	    rintro W' g ⟨A, hA, hWA, hAV⟩
256	    haveI : Subsingleton (ToType (Q.obj (Opposite.op A))) :=
257	      AddCommGrpCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (hSec A hA)
258	    have hgfac : g.op = (homOfLE hAV).op ≫ (homOfLE hWA).op := by
259	      rw [← op_comp]; congr 1
260	    rw [hgfac, Q.map_comp, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
261	      Subsingleton.elim ((ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map (homOfLE hAV).op)) s) 0, map_zero]
262	
263	end AffineHomAcyclic

265	/-! ## Affineness of the intersection opens of an affine cover of a separated scheme -/
266	
267	/-- A finite (nonempty-indexed) infimum of affine opens of a separated scheme is affine.
268	Induction on the index bound via `IsAffineOpen.inf` (which needs the affine diagonal of `X`,
269	available since `X` is separated). Project-local helper. -/
270	private lemma isAffineOpen_iInf_fin [X.IsSeparated] :
271	    ∀ (p : ℕ) (W : Fin (p + 1) → X.Opens), (∀ k, IsAffineOpen (W k)) →
272	      IsAffineOpen (⨅ k, W k) := by
273	  haveI : IsClosedImmersion (pullback.diagonal (terminal.from X)) :=
274	    IsSeparated.isClosedImmersion_diagonal
275	  intro p
276	  induction p with
277	  | zero =>
278	    intro W hW
279	    have heq : (⨅ k, W k) = W 0 :=
280	      le_antisymm (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iInf fun k =>
281	        le_of_eq (congrArg W (Fin.ext (Nat.lt_one_iff.mp k.isLt)).symm))
282	    rw [heq]; exact hW 0
283	  | succ p ih =>
284	    intro W hW
285	    have hsplit : (⨅ k, W k) = W 0 ⊓ ⨅ k : Fin (p + 1), W k.succ := by
286	      refine le_antisymm (le_inf (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iInf fun k => iInf_le _ k.succ))
287	        (le_iInf fun k => ?_)
288	      rcases Fin.eq_zero_or_eq_succ k with hk | ⟨j, hj⟩
289	      · subst hk; exact inf_le_left
290	      · subst hj; exact inf_le_of_right_le (iInf_le _ j)
291	    rw [hsplit]
292	    exact (hW 0).inf (ih (fun k => W k.succ) (fun k => hW k.succ))
293	
294	/-- The intersection open `U_σ = U_{σ 0} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{σ p}` of an affine open cover of a separated
295	scheme is affine. Project-local helper for `cechTerm_pushforward_acyclic`. -/
296	lemma isAffineOpen_coverInterOpen [X.IsSeparated] (𝒰 : X.OpenCover)
297	    (h𝒰 : ∀ i, IsAffine (𝒰.X i)) {p : ℕ} (σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) :
298	    IsAffineOpen (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) := by
299	  have hco : ∀ i, IsAffineOpen (coverOpen 𝒰 i) := by
300	    intro i
301	    haveI : IsAffine (𝒰.X i) := h𝒰 i
302	    exact isAffineOpen_opensRange (𝒰.f i)
303	  exact isAffineOpen_iInf_fin p (fun k => coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)) (fun k => hco (σ k))

513	/-- **Presentation of a restriction from an over-presentation** (general-opens port of
514	`presentationModulesRestrictBasicOpen` minus the affine identification step): if `M.over U`
515	carries a presentation and `W ≤ U`, the subscheme restriction `M.restrict W.ι` admits a global

/-- **The Čech complex computes the higher direct images** (Stacks Tag 02KE;
blueprint `lem:cech_computes_cohomology_affineCover`).

Let `f : X ⟶ S` be a separated quasi-compact morphism with `X` and `S` both separated, `F` a
quasi-coherent `O_X`-module, `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X` (with all cover opens affine,
